# errkit

Tiny CLI that owns a generated TypeScript error catalog.

```bash
npx errkit add USER_NOT_AUTHORIZED --description "User not authorized"
```

Produces / updates `errors.ts`:

```ts
// AUTO-GENERATED BY ERRKIT. DO NOT EDIT MANUALLY.
// This file is managed by errkit.

export enum Err {
  /** User not authorized */
  USER_NOT_AUTHORIZED = "K7M2QP",
}
```

## Usage

```
errkit add NAME [--description TEXT] [-o FILE]
```

- `NAME` — uppercase snake case, matches `^[A-Z][A-Z0-9_]*$`
- `--description`, `-d` — optional JSDoc text
- `--output`, `-o` — explicit target file. Otherwise errkit walks up from the cwd
  looking for an `errors.ts` containing the errkit marker comment, and falls back
  to `./errors.ts`.

## Behavior

- Only modifies files that begin with the errkit marker comment.
- Generates a 6-character code from a human-safe alphabet (no `I`, `L`, `O`, `0`, `1`).
- Regenerates the full file on each write, sorted by name.
- Refuses duplicate names; regenerates on code collisions.

## Development

```bash
bun install
bun test
```
